summary: The conjecture that the minimum number of lazy transpositions in a 2-uniformity network is 2n-3 was posed in arXiv:2208.06630 (published in DMTCS 2025); the authors supply a matching construction and note an analogy with selection networks. No follow-up paper proving or disproving the conjecture was found in a broad web search. The companion paper arXiv:2208.06629 (same authors, same submission date) studies a related but distinct problem—perfect shuffling by lazy transpositions whose composition is uniform—and does not address the 2-uniformity lower bound.
search_queries: lazy transpositions 2-uniformity network reachability Groenland Johnston Radcliffe Scott
arXiv 2208.06630 Short reachability networks lazy transpositions conjecture
perfect shuffling lazy transpositions 2-uniformity Groenland Johnston 2024 2025
arXiv Perfect shuffling with fewer lazy transpositions Groenland Johnston
notes: No follow-up found after searching multiple queries. The paper was first posted August 2022 and published in DMTCS in October 2025. The companion paper arXiv:2208.06629 by the same authors concerns a different notion (lazy transpositions composing to a uniform permutation) and does not resolve Conjecture 1. The conjecture appears to remain open.
